definition: mean of the highest one-third of individual wave heights measured in a given period of time

definition_ml.en: mean of the highest one-third of individual wave heights measured in a given period of time

source_document: Federal Maritime and Hydrographical Agency, Germany
note: The random nature of surface waves implies that individual waves can be substantially higher than the significant wave height. Highest individual waves in a typical storm with typical duration are approximately two times the significant wave height.

note_ml.en: The random nature of surface waves implies that individual waves can be substantially higher than the significant wave height. Highest individual waves in a typical storm with typical duration are approximately two times the significant wave height.

remark: Waves higher than roughly twice the significant wave height fall into the category of freak or rogue waves

remark_ml.en: Waves higher than roughly twice the significant wave height fall into the category of freak or rogue waves
